subject: ap-psychology
title: Research Methods & Experimental Design
stimulus:
Claire’s sports team made it to the championship basketball game. To play the game, the teammates will need
to work together to move a ball toward their own goal. They must then aim carefully to throw the ball into a
hoop that is elevated about 10 feet off the ground. The winner is the team that gets the ball into the hoop the
most times.

parts.prompt: Explain how Retinal disparity relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that the difference in visual signals from each of Claire’s eyes allows her to see depth, which helps her be successful during the game.
Acceptable: Claire can see depth because she sees two slightly different images out of each eye,

parts.prompt: Explain how Intrinsic motivation relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that Claire has some specific kind of internal motivation instead of external motivation that helps her to be successful during the basketball game.
Acceptable: Claire is motivated by doing her best, so she does well in the game because of her good; Claire is motivated by her personal goal to be the best player in the game.

parts.prompt: Explain how Social facilitation relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that Claire’s success in the game is helped by the physical presence of others.
Acceptable: Claire is really energized by the presence of the crowd at the game, so she does better; Claire is really energized by the presence of the crowd at the game, so she plays better

parts.prompt: Explain how Kinesthetic sense relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that Claire’s sensation of the movement of her body and/or the position of her body part(s) is related to her success in the game.
Acceptable: Claire’s ability to know where her hands are in relation to other parts of her body will; Claire’s awareness of how her hand moves as she dribbles and shoots helps her score

parts.prompt: Claire’s team won the championship game. Ten years later, the team got together for a reunion at the gym where the game was originally held. At the reunion, they discussed the championship game. Explain how Context-dependent memory relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that Claire remembers more about the game because she is in the same physical location where she encoded the memory.
Acceptable: Claire remembered a lot of information about the game because she was in the same

parts.prompt: Claire’s team won the championship game. Ten years later, the team got together for a reunion at the gym where the game was originally held. At the reunion, they discussed the championship game. Explain how Self-serving bias relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that Claire takes credit for events with positive outcomes and/or blames others for negative events in her recollection of the game.
Acceptable: As Claire remembers the game, she takes credit for the team winning.; As Claire remembers the game, she blames the referees for her fouling out of the game.

parts.prompt: Claire’s team won the championship game. Ten years later, the team got together for a reunion at the gym where the game was originally held. At the reunion, they discussed the championship game. Explain how Retroactive interference relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that a recent memory is preventing Claire from remembering something about the championship game.
Acceptable: Claire couldn’t remember what number she had in the championship game because she; Claire couldn’t recall the exact final score in the Championship game because she
